claim: Anthropic officially ships 'Claude Code in Slack' which auto-routes @Claude mentions in channels into a Claude Code on the web session, posts status updates back to the Slack thread, and offers Code-only or Code+Chat routing modes; available on Pro/Max/Team/Enterprise.
quote: Claude Code in Slack brings the power of Claude Code directly into your Slack workspace. When you mention @Claude with a coding task, Claude automatically detects the intent and creates a Claude Code session on the web... as the Claude Code session progresses, Claude posts status updates back to your Slack thread

claim: Anthropic launched Claude Code in Slack as a research preview on December 8, 2025, framed as 'delegate tasks to Claude Code directly from Slack' rather than a webhook hack.
quote: Now in beta as a research preview, Claude makes it easy to move context from Slack conversations to coding sessions... When you mention @Claude in Slack, Claude reviews your message to determine if it's a coding task. If it is, a new Claude Code session will automatically be created.

claim: The 'Claude' app on the Slack App Marketplace is the official Anthropic-published consumer app — the same app surface that Claude Code in Slack is built on top of.
quote: Transform your work day with Claude, your thoughtful AI collaborator in Slack. Draft an email, summarize a document, brainstorm ideas, or get fast answers.

claim: The 'Claude in Slack' help-center article frames the integration as Claude.ai chat plus Claude Code routing — i.e. it is both the consumer chat surface and the dev-task entry point, not two separate apps.
quote: Claude in Slack gives you AI assistance right where your team collaborates.

claim: Anthropic's Claude Code Routines (research preview, April 14 2026) are the official 'remote-trigger Claude Code from outside' surface and natively support Schedule, API (per-routine bearer-token /fire endpoint), and GitHub-event triggers — but Slack is not a first-class trigger; Slack appears only as an MCP connector for the routine to read/write during a run.
quote: Each routine can have one or more triggers attached to it: Scheduled... API: trigger on demand by sending an HTTP POST to a per-routine endpoint with a bearer token... GitHub: run automatically in response to repository events

claim: anthropics/claude-code-action v1 is officially triggered by GitHub events (issue_comment, pull_request_review_comment, issues opened/assigned, schedule, workflow_dispatch) and reacts to '@claude' mentions; the docs show no first-party Slack trigger — Slack-to-GHA bridges remain user-built via repository_dispatch.
quote: on: issue_comment: types: [created] pull_request_review_comment: types: [created] issues: types: [opened, assigned] ... Responds to @claude mentions in comments

claim: The official anthropics/claude-code-action repo (7,317 stars) is the supported GitHub Action and supports Anthropic API, AWS Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, and Microsoft Foundry as backends — but its trigger surface is GitHub events, not Slack.
quote: A general-purpose Claude Code action for GitHub PRs and issues that can answer questions and implement code changes. This action intelligently detects when to activate based on your workflow context—whether responding to @claude mentions, issue assignments, or executing automation tasks with explicit prompts.

claim: Slack itself (not Anthropic) ships and maintains the official Slack MCP server, GA on February 17 2026; Anthropic is a partner consumer of it and the connector is what Claude.ai/Claude Code uses to read/write Slack.
quote: We continue to deepen our integration with Slack through our MCP Apps extension and by including the power of Claude Code.

claim: The original modelcontextprotocol/servers-archived reference Slack server (parent repo 84,644 stars) was archived May 29, 2025; @modelcontextprotocol/server-slack on npm is no longer the canonical path — Slack's own MCP server superseded it.
quote: This repository was archived by the owner on May 29, 2025. It is now read-only.
